title_en: The Sugar Swap That Changed Everything
title_es: El Cambio de Azúcar que Lo Cambió Todo
hook_en: Did you know the 1980 guidelines replaced fat with sugar?
hook_es: ¿Sabías que las guías de 1980 reemplazaron la grasa con azúcar?
key_message: The 1980 US Dietary Guidelines promoted a low-fat diet, leading to sugar and additive replacements that fueled obesity.
visual_elements: comparison of labels: low-fat vs. full-fat
graph showing obesity rates from 1980 onwards
sugar and additives being added to processed food
_source_regulation_context:
KEY REGULATORY EVENTS:
  • 1980: First US Dietary Guidelines issued — Impact: Promoted low-fat diet; food industry began replacing fat with sugar and additives

US OBESITY RATES OVER TIME:
  • 1960: 13.4% (adults), 5.0% (children)
  • 1980: 15.0% (adults), 5.5% (children)
  • 1994: 22.5% (adults), 10.0% (children)
  • 2000: 30.5% (adults), 13.9% (children)
  • 2010: 35.7% (adults), 16.9% (children)
  • 2016: 39.8% (adults), 18.5% (children)
  • 2020: 41.9% (adults), 19.7% (children)
  • 2024: 43.0% (adults), 21.0% (children)

title_en: Why Are Certain Additives Banned Abroad?
title_es: ¿Por Qué Se Prohíben Aditivos en Otros Países?
hook_en: These additives are banned globally, but not in the US!
hook_es: ¡Estos aditivos están prohibidos en todo el mundo, pero no en EE.UU.!
key_message: Some additives banned in other countries are still permitted in the US, impacting health and obesity rates.
visual_elements: list of banned additives in other countries
comparison of food labels: US vs. EU
animation of world map highlighting where additives are banned
visual of health effects linked to additives
props_needed: food labels from US and EU
